thesis: search unsearched nonce gaps — same solve rate, zero historical competition

dense_zone.notes: Historically exhausted region — where most miners search

phi15_multiples.notes: Replicates observed miner clustering around φ^15

gap_seeded.notes: Largest identified unsearched gap — zero historical competition

gap_phi_tiled.notes: φ-tiled low-discrepancy coverage within the gap

verdict:
--- VERDICT ---
✅ gap_seeded hit rate (201.6) ≈ uniform_random (199.2) within 10%.
   SHA-256d is memoryless — the gap is NOT harder to solve.
   Economic thesis CONFIRMED: searching the gap costs the same but faces
   ZERO historical competition (gap = 367,634,399 nonces, never mined).

Key insight: The advantage is NOT in SHA-256d structure.
It IS in nonce space occupancy: 367,634,399 nonces in the largest
identified gap have never been mined. Same expected solve rate, zero crowding.
